nativeRange.habitat: Generally found on offshore reefs and reef slopes at depths of roughly 10–70 m, often associated with soft-bottom or muddy substrates with rubble ridges and rich invertebrate growth, especially sponges

decorPreferences: open swimming space for fin display

diet.notes: Tends to feed on live shrimp, small fish, and crustacean flesh. When first introduced, live saltwater feeder shrimp are generally advised to encourage feeding. With patience, many individuals have been known to transition to silversides, frozen shrimp, and other meaty foods, often via target feeding with a feeding stick. Ornamental shrimp and small fish in the aquarium tend to be at risk of predation.

avoidWith: small fish that fit in mouth

summary: The Mombasa Lionfish (Pterois mombasae) is a deeper-water Indian Ocean lionfish prized for its frilled pectoral fins and bold reddish-brown banding. Generally considered intermediate care; tends to be nocturnal and best housed in a dimly lit FOWLR system with ample caves.
careNotes:
The Mombasa Lionfish, sometimes called the Frillfin Turkeyfish, is a deeper-reef species native primarily to the Western Indian Ocean — East Africa (Kenya, Tanzania, Mozambique), the Maldives, and Sri Lanka — with its range extending east through the Indo-West Pacific to southern Japan and New Caledonia. In the wild it is typically found at depths of 15–40 m on offshore reef slopes and rubble bottoms with rich invertebrate growth, particularly sponges.

Venomous spines: The dorsal, pelvic, and anal fin spines are venomous. Handlers should exercise caution; stings have been reported as intensely painful, and treatment with hot water (as hot as can be tolerated) plus medical attention is generally advised.

Lighting and tank setup: Because this species comes from deeper water, it generally appreciates a dimly lit aquarium with ample shaded overhangs, live-rock caves, and crevices to rest in during the day. Open swimming space is also helpful to accommodate the dramatic fin display when the fish is active.

Feeding transition: Newly imported specimens often refuse prepared foods. Live feeder shrimp are commonly used to initiate feeding, after which most individuals can be coaxed onto frozen silversides, krill, and shrimp via target-feeding with a feeding stick.

Reef compatibility: Generally considered reef-safe with caution. It tends to leave corals and sessile invertebrates alone but will readily eat ornamental shrimp and small fish, so placement in mixed-reef systems with vulnerable invertebrates warrants careful consideration.

Cross-source notes: Adult size figures vary across sources — LiveAquaria states 6.25 in (~15.9 cm), fishy.wiki gives 15.7 cm, and FishBase reports 20 cm SL as the maximum; the 12–17 cm range above reflects the consensus midpoint. Minimum tank size is commonly stated as 50 US gal (~190 L) by retailers, but hobby write-ups recommend 75–100 US gal (280–380 L) for full adult lifetime housing; 200 L is used as a cautious minimum on the nice-values list.
breedingNotes: Pelagic broadcast spawning, similar to other Pterois species; courtship and spawning have rarely been documented in detail for this species, and captive breeding is not known to occur outside of specialized public-aquarium settings.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Source URL list cited the collection page; the species-specific product page was used for parameter extraction. Confirmed care level (moderate), temperament (semi-aggressive), reef compatibility (with caution), temperature 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, adult size 6.25 in, minimum tank 50 US gal, native range Indian Ocean (East Africa, Maldives, Sri Lanka).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Scorpaenidae), max size (20 cm SL), depth range (10–70 m), temperature (24–28.6°C, mean 27.2°C), distribution (east coast of Africa east to New Caledonia, north to southern Japan), and habitat (offshore reefs over soft/rubble bottoms with sponge-rich invertebrate growth). IUCN status: Least Concern.

additional.name: fishy.wiki
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size 15.7 cm, minimum tank 189 L (50 US gal), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, care level moderate, semi-aggressive temperament, reef safe 'with caution'.

additional.name: TankFacts
additional.notes: Confirmed temperature 76–80°F (24.4–26.7°C), pH 7.8–8.4 (wider than other sources), salinity 1.023–1.026, dKH 8–12. Listed reef-safe as 'No' rather than 'with caution'; majority 'with caution' was used.

fish.breedingNotes: Pelagic broadcast spawning suspected as with congeners; captive breeding has not been meaningfully documented outside specialized public aquaria.
